title: Semiaquilegia eastwoodiae
intro: Semiaquilegia eastwoodiae, also known as Eastwood's columbine, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the mountains of New Mexico, USA. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throat, and respiratory infections, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Semiaquilegia eastwoodiae include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Eastwood's columbine include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory issues.
